method: Strategy B (Master Assistant cluster) + reverse-chain climb from POLLER/SECONDARY common callers
key_discovery: 0xEABDB0 is an UNDOCUMENTED second common caller of BOTH the poller (0xEAD3E0) and the apply path (0xEAD930). Live findings only named 0x166CA90. Climbing 0xEABDB0 upward yields a single-parent pipeline whose root caller is a Master-Assistant-string-referencing function (0xD58A20), reachable from a call-tree root (0xD572F0) = the GUI/IPC entry surface.

verified_pipeline.role: TRIGGER: refs Master Assistant string; 1 caller; 22 callees incl pipeline root; thiscall context-struct prologue

evidence: one of 20 funcs with direct RIP xref to Master Assistant string
22 callees incl 0xe9fc30 (verified pipeline root to POLLER+SECONDARY)
prologue: rcx saved as context/this, sub rsp,0x7e0, reads [rcx] vtable + [this+0x90] flag byte = start-analysis entry shape
does NOT directly call state helpers -> reaches them via the 6-stage pipeline (matches the 0-direct-xref phase-string observation in recon)
verified pipeline root (calls 0xea1b20 which chains to HUB)
smaller; more pipeline-internal than the MA-string entry; could be a sub-phase kickoff rather than the GUI entry

trigger_candidate_tertiary.role: call-tree root above 0xd58a20; candidate for the actual GUI Play-button dispatch (the wrapper that selects assistant target then calls 0xd58a20)
evidence: sole caller of the MA-string entry 0xd58a20
needs prologue check to confirm GUI/IPC boundary

rejected.0x166ca90: documented caller; only calls POLLER+SECONDARY directly, no pipeline, NOT the trigger (confirmed by live findings)
rejected.0xead3e0: poller, 548 calls/min idle, returns 0x1 (confirmed NOT trigger)
rejected.0xead930: apply path (param mutation), not the start trigger
rejected.0xfd7f30: SmoothAudioDataStream ctor; audio ingestion layer, separate subtree (callers 0xfbd0b0/0x1072860) with no bridge to the control pipeline in the 1-hop graph

thunk_finding.disasm: push rbx; sub rsp,0x20; add rcx,8; mov rbx,rdx; call 0xd58a20; mov rax,rbx; ret
thunk_finding.interpretation: MSVC this-pointer-adjustor THUNK (vtable slot). +8 this adjustment = virtual-method dispatch into a base sub-object. This is the vtable entry the GUI Play button calls; the actual trigger body is 0xD58A20.
thunk_finding.implication: The trigger is invoked through a VIRTUAL call (call qword ptr [rax+N]), so static callers-list for 0xD58A20 shows only the thunk 0xD572F0; the real GUI/IPC caller reaches it indirectly via the vtable. This explains the single-parent chain and the call-tree-root profile.
